current_runtime_observation.interpretation: The official paper ledger is still negative in aggregate; no strategy is live-ready.

hypotheses.name: Dynamic grid as range-capture engine
hypotheses.hypothesis: A cost-aware adaptive grid can capture repeated mean-reverting moves on liquid EUR crypto pairs when spread is tight, volatility is sufficient, and trend-breakout risk is controlled.
hypotheses.timeframe: Runtime tick stream with rolling price windows; no fixed candle backtest yet
hypotheses.entry_logic: Buy near selected grid levels after opportunity scoring, cost guard, ATR/regime checks, pair health guard, and strategy governance allow official paper entry.
hypotheses.exit_logic: Sell at grid profit target, trailing ATR take-profit, smart recenter close, or emergency/grid invalidation close.
hypotheses.risk_model: Per-symbol exposure caps, per-order sizing caps, max positions, pair health guard, opportunity score gate, no live auto-promotion.
hypotheses.fees_model: Official paper ledger stores execution fees; shadow grid uses configurable per-side fee bps and includes round-trip fees in net PnL.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Shadow grid applies configurable per-side slippage bps; official ledger records estimated slippage bps where available but lacks full queue/partial-fill modeling.
failure_modes: shadow setup diverges from official paper fills

baseline_comparison.current_status: no standardized event-driven baseline report yet

hypotheses.decision_reason: Grid/dynamic_grid is legacy research-only after official paper evidence showed negative aggregate performance after costs; it must not route runtime orders or write new official paper ledger rows.

hypotheses.name: Trend and volatility breakout engine
hypotheses.hypothesis: Trend-following variants can outperform grid behavior during directional, expanding-volatility regimes if entries are delayed until momentum exceeds fees, spread and slippage.
hypotheses.timeframe: Paper-only shadow tick windows, typically 10 second throttled updates and rolling price samples
hypotheses.entry_logic: Shadow variants test Donchian breakout or EMA momentum confirmation when momentum and ATR thresholds pass.
hypotheses.exit_logic: ATR trailing stop, stop ATR multiple, exit-window reversal, or strategy-specific stop.
hypotheses.risk_model: Shadow-only virtual capital per variant, position_pct per variant, cooldown ticks, no official live routing.
hypotheses.fees_model: Trend shadow lab subtracts configurable per-side fee bps from entry and exit.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Trend shadow lab subtracts configurable per-side slippage bps from entry and exit.

baseline_comparison.current_status: shadow lab exists; standardized baseline report missing

hypotheses.decision_reason: No sufficient official paper or walk-forward evidence yet; keep it as a shadow producer until robust samples exist.

hypotheses.name: Mean reversion snapback engine
hypotheses.hypothesis: Bollinger/z-score snapback variants can capture temporary deviations in range-like markets when trend strength is capped and expected snapback exceeds costs.
hypotheses.timeframe: Paper-only shadow tick windows with rolling sample buffers
hypotheses.entry_logic: Enter shadow long when z-score deviation, bandwidth, ATR, and expected net edge filters pass.
hypotheses.exit_logic: Exit near mean reversion threshold, z-score exit, or stop z-score.
hypotheses.risk_model: Shadow-only virtual capital per variant, position_pct per variant, cooldown ticks, trend-strength cap.
hypotheses.fees_model: Mean reversion shadow lab subtracts configurable per-side fee bps from entry and exit.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Mean reversion shadow lab subtracts configurable per-side slippage bps from entry and exit.

baseline_comparison.current_status: shadow lab exists; standardized baseline report missing

hypotheses.decision_reason: The production class is explicitly disabled and shadow evidence has not passed workflow gates.

hypotheses.name: High conviction multi-timeframe swing research engine
hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-timeframe trend, breakout, pullback, volatility expansion and asymmetric MFE/MAE setups may produce fewer but larger spot-only long opportunities than micro-grid signals when validated after costs and walk-forward splits.
hypotheses.timeframe: Research-only OHLCV windows using 5m entries, 15m structure, 1h trend and 4h context when available
hypotheses.entry_logic: Research signal discovery only: detect high-potential long-only swing setups from OHLCV multi-timeframe context; no runtime order is created.
hypotheses.exit_logic: Research simulation uses stop-loss, take-profit, trailing stop, time stop and trend invalidation variants.
hypotheses.risk_model: Portfolio-aware research replay with initial capital, realized-equity sizing, one position per symbol, cooldown, max exposure, max daily loss and drawdown stop.
hypotheses.fees_model: Research runs must include paper_current_taker or research_stress cost profiles.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Research runs must include configured spread/slippage and stress-cost comparison until real bid/ask/depth coverage is sufficient.

baseline_comparison.current_status: research-only walk-forward reports exist; not eligible for official paper without stronger sample and gate evidence

hypotheses.decision_reason: High Conviction exists only in research/orchestrator reports and has not passed the P0 promotion gates for official paper capital.

hypotheses.name: Relative value statistical arbitrage proxy
hypotheses.hypothesis: Long-only proxy trades on statistically undervalued Kraken spot assets versus correlated references or baskets may identify research opportunities without shorting the reference asset.
hypotheses.entry_logic: Research signal only: generate long-only BUY proxy when target asset is statistically undervalued versus its reference relationship.
hypotheses.exit_logic: Research simulation exits on z-score mean reversion, fixed TP/SL, time stop, or relationship invalidation.
hypotheses.risk_model: No capital allocation while status remains rejected/no-go; any future review must use portfolio-aware exposure caps and one position per symbol.
hypotheses.fees_model: Research runs must include paper_current_taker and research_stress costs.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Research runs must include spread/slippage stress and reject missing-cost evidence.
failure_modes: reference asset cannot be shorted on spot
spread does not revert after costs

baseline_comparison.current_status: last research verdict was NO GO after costs

hypotheses.decision_reason: Relative Value remains NO GO/research-only after current validation; it must not receive paper capital or runtime execution.

hypotheses.name: Opportunity scoring and allocation layer
hypotheses.hypothesis: Combining gross edge, cost, ATR, spread, liquidity, signal stability, regime and pair health can improve selection by ranking opportunities before risk/execution.
hypotheses.timeframe: Runtime snapshot from latest signal/decision context
hypotheses.entry_logic: Does not generate entries by itself; ranks whether a signal should remain tradable and recommends paper allocation.
hypotheses.exit_logic: No exit logic; downstream strategies and execution govern exits.
hypotheses.risk_model: Score threshold, per-symbol and total exposure caps, paper/live gate separation, pair health adjustment.
hypotheses.fees_model: Consumes edge_context total cost bps and fee bps produced by signal/cost logic.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Consumes edge_context slippage bps and spread bps; does not simulate fills.
failure_modes: missing order book creates stale spread
pair health penalizes before enough sample
hypotheses.decision_reason: It is a guard/ranking layer, not a standalone alpha; it needs ablation against no-score routing.

hypotheses.name: Markov/entropy regime scoring
hypotheses.hypothesis: A lightweight entropy plus Markov-state sensor can improve routing by detecting range, trend, chaos, high-volatility and low-activity contexts without acting as a price predictor.
hypotheses.timeframe: Rolling runtime price history, default entropy window 64 and Markov window 96
hypotheses.entry_logic: Does not create entries; applies a bounded score adjustment to opportunity ranking.
hypotheses.risk_model: Bounded adjustment by config; never a blocker by itself.
hypotheses.fees_model: Not applicable directly; downstream opportunity/cost guard models fees.
hypotheses.slippage_model: Not applicable directly; downstream opportunity/cost guard models slippage.
failure_modes: state persistence not predictive of next trade

baseline_comparison.current_status: sensor implemented; incremental value not yet proven

hypotheses.decision_reason: Useful as observability and ranking context, but not validated as an alpha source.

hypotheses.name: No-trade safety baseline
hypotheses.hypothesis: Abstaining is a valid baseline and should beat negative-expectancy trading after costs.
hypotheses.entry_logic: Do not enter when evidence, cost, regime or governance gates are insufficient.
hypotheses.decision_reason: No-trade is the required safety baseline while official paper evidence remains weak.

hypotheses.name: Triangular arbitrage prototype
hypotheses.hypothesis: Triangular cross-rate inconsistencies could produce net profit after three legs of fees if order book depth, latency, and fill risk are modeled.
hypotheses.market: Crypto cross pairs, not currently validated for Kraken EUR paper stack
hypotheses.entry_logic: Prototype detects triangular price inconsistency above minimum net profit.
hypotheses.exit_logic: Atomic multi-leg completion; not implemented as production execution.
hypotheses.risk_model: Not production-ready; multi-leg execution risk not controlled.
hypotheses.decision_reason: Requires synchronized depth and atomic multi-leg execution modeling before any paper/live route.
